transcript: Hi Vogue, my name is Priscilla Alexandra Spring. I'm the Leather Goods Creative Director at Hermès. We're in Pantin, near Paris, and you'll be spending the day with me today, so let's go. We have a very strong heritage and a really beautiful history. I hope that when you buy a bag from Hermès, it's love at first sight, but also something that you know you're gonna hold for a long time. So this is our studio, our Leather Goods studio where the team works and we're very excited to share with you because it's the first time we open our door. I'll take you to my office i grew up in this environment of like with my family always working with their hands and myself i've always loved to draw and that was really what my passion was as a child and i spent hours and hours hours and hours sketching. My grandma was an embroiderer, and she always taught me how the most beautiful embroidery is the reverse, as it should be as beautiful as the outside. And my dad was a self-taught creative, so I always watched him doing a lot of painting, sculpture. Usually I do little sketches sketches just to explain more or less what the idea is and then it's gonna be a discussion with the maquettist and we're going to try to make a 3D maquette, then if we like it we'll move on to leather. Pri? I don't know. Do you have five seconds? Of course. So it's gonna be right on time for the show. Yeah, perfect. And Nadege took several ones. Okay, cool. They did it in salpa, right? Yeah, yeah, yeah. To explain, this is the salpa. It's a material like a fabric that we use that simulates leather because it really behaves like leather. The good thing is that we can make several mock-ups, as you can see, and we can play with it before actually using the real leather. And it really helps us to visualize how things are going to look in the end, as you can see. Thank you. Thank later salut hi how are you good you good how's it going what's working as we talked about before on hermes backpack the idea is to bring a new gestuality something a little bit more contemporary but still with the very recognizable codes of hermes there's different ways to in our collections. You know, we have a very strong heritage and a really beautiful history, which is very inspiring. But we also like to bring new ideas to the table that are based on our classics, like the Kelly, the Birkin, or the Oua Courroie. We have these old equestrian catalogs, either by the shape, a strap, a form. It's endless, the way we can be inspired by these old catalogs. It's anelier. And basically she's the head of development and she makes sure that all the bags that leave her mess uh they have to be validated by christine 40 years she knows everything she is the queen that's how we call her queen christine Voilà, la reine. Oui, je comprends ça. Hello! the queen. That's how we call her. Queen Christine. Voilà. La reine. Oui, je comprends ça. Hello. Salut Alexandre, ça va? Bonjour. Salut Aurélie. Hello. Ça va? Ça va et vous? Alors, ça se passe comment ce petit moraillon? Eh bien, ça se passe très bien. Du coup, celui-ci, il est monté et il et prêt à être cousu. holes and Aurélie is going to stitch everything by hand so that we can then close our bag. This bag was designed and we did it in the studio for like maybe three months and now with the bureau des études maybe we're gonna take like about six to nine months to put it a point okay so now we're going to see stephen he's working with alexandre that is from the bureau des orfevres which means the hardware workshop and they're trying to get the metal and the leather working together the magic of this bag is inspired by a gigantic horse bit the idea is to hide the whole metal part with the leather. We polish all our hardware by hand and we use brass. We love noise at Hermès, the noise that the metal does and the noise that a closure has. This is little details that we pay a lot of attention as well that most people don't know and we do spend a little time on just hearing the noise and see if we like the noise. Salut, Karine. Bonjour. Bonjour, ça va? So this is the front of a Kelly. Because we like the client not to feel anything in the back when you touch your back inside, we make a little hole where we will put this little closure. Our artisans, they need to do four years of training to be able to manufacture a kelly bag. This that you've just seen, the perlage, you need to be very skilled and very, very good because if you miss one single second to the side, you have to redo everything again. So bravo Karine! Bonjour Fabrice! Fabrice is doing the last stitching on a Kelly bag. A Kelly takes around 13 hours to be made depending on the materials that we make it i like the kelly because it's an archetype it was made in the 30s and it was always in the shelf until grace kelly one day covered their pregnant belly from paparazzi and then that was it the rest is the history the kelly became the kelly so as you can see that we built the kelly retournée it's made inside out it requires a lot of skill to turn it and get it on the right way this is our stock of leathers that we usually use to do our mock-ups. We have about 200 colors, always in collection, and about 50 materials, including different leathers as you can see. Craftsmanship is really at the core of the Maison. If for anything, it's the work of women and men that they do by hand. It's been transmitted for many generations now, so we do have our specific savoir-faire, the way we stitch, the way we assemble, the way we think about bags at Hermès addresses longevity from the very beginning. We think straight away, how are we going to repair this? How someone in maybe 30 years will repair this? So I think this addresses sustainability in a very different way because also the ultimate goal with our bags is that they last as long time as possible. Let me introduce you to Mark. Mark has been working at Hermes for 25 years and he's just doing a Birkin picnic and he's going to show us how he's doing the little lace. So to assemble the bag, the leather and the wicker, everything is a little hand massage. It takes about 8 hours to do the lacing all around the bag, as you can see Mark already started inside good day today with us here at Hermès. I hope you learned a lot about how we do things, and it's night time, so time to go home. See you soon
